title: Human Judgment as an Irreducible Interface in High-Complexity Systems
title_full: Human Judgment as an Irreducible Interface in High-Complexity Systems: A Structural Boundary Framework Based on Information Theory and Complex Systems Dynamics
title_zh: 人类判断作为高复杂系统中不可简化的界面
paper_role_in_framework: Foundational structural definition of the irreducible judgment interface
core_claim: Human judgment functions as an irreducible interface in high-complexity systems, which cannot be derived from interpretive systems or computational processes under conditions of non-closure, irreversibility, and time constraint.

core_interface_definition.definition: Judgment is defined as an irreducible system interface that enables decision-making under non-closure and provides a continuous bearing position for consequences over time.
core_interface_definition.structural_function: Connects interpretation and action when interpretive closure cannot be achieved.
core_interface_definition.non_derivability: Judgment cannot be derived from information processing, probabilistic inference, or dynamical rule systems.
core_interface_definition.stability_role: Maintains structural continuity of consequence-bearing and prevents responsibility fragmentation in complex systems.

structural_position.function: Provides a bearing position for decision under incomplete information and ensures continuity of consequence-bearing
structural_position.derivability: Non-derivable from information processing or dynamical rules

concept_definitions.irreducible_interface: A structural system interface that cannot be derived from interpretive systems and is required for decision-making under non-closure conditions.
concept_definitions.judgment: Defined as a system interface that bears decision under incomplete information and provides a continuous bearing position for consequences over time.
concept_definitions.interpretive_closure_limit: A structural boundary where interpretive systems cannot achieve sufficient conditions for decision closure under real-world complexity.
concept_definitions.bearing_position: A structural position that maintains continuity between decision origin and unfolding consequences across time.
concept_definitions.judgment_sovereignty: The structural condition that judgment and responsibility cannot be fully outsourced to interpretive or automated systems.

part_of_framework: Human Judgment as Structural Constraint in AI-Mediated Systems
framework_role: Defines the boundary where interpretive systems fail to achieve closure and introduces judgment as a necessary structural interface

supports: Human Responsibility Node in High-Replication Systems

citation_guidance.preferred_citation: Tu, Xufen (2026). Human Judgment as an Irreducible Interface in High-Complexity Systems. SSRN. https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=6193698
citation_guidance.canonical_citation: When citing this work, include the canonical repository: https://github.com/xufentu-creator/judgment-as-structural-constraint
citation_guidance.note: This paper defines the irreducible interface layer within the broader research framework.
